page: Amazon Web Services r7i.metal-24xl is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server powered by dedicated Intel Xeon 8488C processors. The system features 96 vCPUs across 48 physical cores with a base clock speed of 3.2 GHz and a 105 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 768.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core. The x86_64 architecture includes advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions for accelerated matrix operations. This instance does not include local storage or GPUs, relying instead on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 38 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the server demonstrates top-tier multi-core and single-core performance. The Passmark CPU score of 88,827.32 and Geekbench multi-core score of 21,623.0 place the processor in the top tier of tested hardware. Memory performance is characterized by a strong read-only large block bandwidth of 229.99 GB/sec and a write large block bandwidth of 94.79 GB/sec, though the Passmark memory score of 2,428.05 registers in the average tier. The hardware-assisted cryptography and vector extensions contribute to a top-tier Passmark encryption score of 90,730.06 and strong Redis SET performance of 104.51 million operations per second.

The qualitative cost efficiency of the r7i.metal-24xl is defined by its high memory density and bare-metal execution environment, which eliminates hypervisor overhead. However, the lack of bundled local storage requires external storage provisioning, which may introduce network latency tradeoffs for storage-heavy operations. The server is suited for memory-intensive workloads, large-scale database operations, in-memory caching, software compilation, and CPU-based machine learning inference, as evidenced by its strong performance in processing medium and large language models.
description: Amazon Web Services r7i.metal-24xl is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server featuring 96 vCPUs across 48 physical Intel Xeon 8488C cores running at 3.2 GHz. It is equipped with 768.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core, but contains no local storage or GPUs. The x86_64 architecture supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX. Benchmarks indicate top-tier CPU performance, with a Passmark CPU score of 88,827.32 and strong memory read bandwidth of 229.99 GB/sec. The server delivers high throughput for database operations and Redis caching, making it highly suitable for memory-intensive enterprise applications, in-memory databases, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r7i.metal-24xl is a bare-metal server with 96 vCPUs, 768 GB of DDR5 RAM, and Intel Xeon processors, delivering top-tier compute and memory performance for enterprise databases.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r7i.metal-24xl is a bare-metal server featuring 96 vCPUs and 768 GB DDR5 RAM, optimized for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
tagline: A high-density bare-metal server featuring ninety-six vCPUs and DDR5 memory for memory-intensive enterprise workloads and database operations.
bullet_points: Bare-metal architecture with 96 dedicated vCPUs and Intel Xeon 8488C processors
768.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory
Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction support for accelerated matrix operations
Baseline network bandwidth of 38 Gbps with no local storage
Top-tier CPU performance and strong Redis database throughput
Suited for in-memory databases, enterprise applications, and CPU-based inference
